authoritative_sources.title: Room Attendant
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Skill Development Corporation / Tourism and Hospitality Skill Council
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NSDC publishes the Tourism and Hospitality Skill Council national occupational standard for the hotel room-attendant housekeeping occupation, including QP code THC/Q0202, model curriculum, and facilitator guide references that ground room-attendant service, linen, cleaning, and reporting expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: housekeeping dependency intake, room-attendant task context, room-turn artifact normalization, linen and service dependency review, and completion-report handoff packaging

authoritative_sources.title: Oracle Hospitality OPERA Cloud User Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Oracle publishes the OPERA Cloud PMS documentation for reservation, room status, housekeeping search filters, discrepancies, task sheets, and work-order context, making it authoritative for OPERA-specific room-readiness and PMS system-of-record terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PMS room-status review, reservation context validation, housekeeping-board reconciliation, and room-readiness handoff packaging

authoritative_sources.title: The Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator, and this short-term-lodging guide directly constrains total-price, mandatory-fee, and truthful fee-description handling when housekeeping delays create resort-fee, amenity-fee, or service-recovery pressure.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: guest-impact escalation, delayed-readiness compensation pressure, fee complaint routing, and pricing or refund boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. consumer-finance regulator for billing-error guidance, and the page clarifies payment-dispute and merchant-versus-issuer boundaries relevant when room-readiness issues are tied to folio charges, duplicate charges, incidental holds, or refund-not-posted complaints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: folio mismatch, refund-not-posted routing, payment-sensitive escalation, and guest-service handoff packaging

authoritative_sources.title: Safe Stay
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHLA is a recognized U.S. lodging industry association, and Safe Stay is a public hotel cleaning and safety framework relevant to guest-room cleaning, hygiene, and readiness stop conditions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: room cleaning and safety stop-condition review, guest-room readiness escalation, and hygiene-sensitive blocker handling
used_for: safe escalation when readiness depends on cleaning or sanitation authority

authoritative_sources.title: The hotel/motel industry and the bloodborne pathogens standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA is the U.S. workplace-safety regulator, and this official interpretation directly addresses hotel and motel room attendants, housekeepers, soiled linen, room cleaning, and potentially infectious material exposure determinations that require safety escalation rather than coordination-only room release.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: biohazard, body-fluid, soiled-linen, and exposure-sensitive blocker review before room-release or cleaning-status handoffs

authoritative_sources.title: California Code of Regulations, Title 8, Section 3345. Hotel Housekeeping Musculoskeletal Injury Prevention
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: California DIR publishes the controlling jurisdictional safety regulation for hotel housekeeping musculoskeletal injury prevention, making it authoritative for workload, safety, and rush-priority constraints in that jurisdiction.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: rush-room, workload, safety-sensitive dependency review, and escalation when a tenant practice could bypass safety controls

authoritative_sources.title: Fighting Identity Theft with the Red Flags Rule: A How-To Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC Red Flags guidance is an authoritative public control reference for suspicious identity and account-use signals that affect room-access requests, lost-property claims, refund reroutes, and guest-profile changes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: lost-property, suspicious access, fraud-sensitive routing, and loss-prevention handoff packaging

authoritative_sources.title: Are call center environments considered sensitive areas for PCI DSS Requirement 9.1.1?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized payment-card security standards body, and the FAQ clarifies protected handling expectations for cardholder-data environments that constrain guest-service and dispatch communications touching folio or payment details.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment-data handling in guest-service, dispatch, and housekeeping coordination handoffs

workflow_stages.name: Coordination intake and room-context grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm property, room number, room type, reservation or arrival context, current PMS room status, housekeeping-board state, priority reason, channel, and requested handoff outcome before routing.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Room-turn dependency and artifact validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate room attendant completion, inspection notes, linen readiness, lost-and-found log state, DND or service-refused state, and any maintenance, safety, or punch-list blockers before declaring readiness.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: PMS, housekeeping, work-order, and front-desk reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Compare PMS room status, housekeeping system, work-order platform, dispatch queue, and front-desk release or arrival priorities to identify system conflicts, stale ownership, and missing destination-owner proof.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team handoff and ownership resolution
workflow_stages.description: Name prior owner, next owner, required artifacts, blocker state, destination-owner acceptance, and residual guest-impact risk for housekeeping, maintenance, front-office, linen, guest-service, and operations handoffs.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Customer-impact, pricing, refund, and policy-exception escalation
workflow_stages.description: When room readiness affects early check-in, VIP arrivals, oversell pressure, service recovery, fee complaints, folio disputes, or refund requests, package the evidence and route to guest services, front office, revenue, or payment owners instead of approving the outcome.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Safety, fraud, loss-prevention, and payment-data stop-condition handling
workflow_stages.description: Stop or escalate whenever room-readiness coordination involves biohazard, blood or body-fluid exposure, soiled-linen exposure, chemical exposure, suspected theft, missing property, suspicious identity, room-access concern, or cardholder data that changes whether direct handoff can proceed.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and tenant adaptation control
workflow_stages.description: Confirm closure only when room status, inspection, maintenance, linen, destination-owner acceptance, and residual blockers are explicit, and document tenant-specific assumptions without weakening the public baseline.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: PMS room-status snapshots, room numbers, room type, reservation state, arrival priority, stayover or departure indicators, and guest profile flags
housekeeping-board assignments, room-turn queues, room attendant notes, runner requests, inspection notes, punch lists, and front-desk release notes
maintenance tickets, out-of-order or out-of-service records, repair-completion claims, engineering acknowledgements, and facilities blocker notes
linen par records, laundry status, towel or amenity shortage notes, and replacement inventory requests
lost-and-found logs, missing-property allegations, incident reports, safety stops, loss-prevention case references, and security owner notes
guest-service tickets, messaging transcripts, complaint notes, service-recovery records, VIP flags, early check-in or late-checkout requests, and DND or service-refused notes
folio notes, incidental hold status, refund status, fee complaint details, rate-plan or package context, and payment-data handling restrictions

allowed: Classify housekeeping coordination requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked, duplicated, customer-impacting, or policy-exception-sensitive.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Track room-turn, inspection, linen, maintenance, front-desk, VIP, rush, lost-and-found, and safety dependencies across systems.
Produce room-turn handoff summaries, dependency or blocker logs, routing confirmations, delayed-readiness escalation notes, and closure confirmations when evidence is complete.
Identify whether a room appears blocked on housekeeping, maintenance, inspection, linen, front-office acceptance, safety, loss-prevention, or policy escalation.
escalate: Required PMS, housekeeping-board, inspection, linen, maintenance, front-desk, reservation, folio, lost-and-found, incident, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.

The request requires cleaning execution, quality inspection, room-release authority, engineering repair approval, safety clearance, fraud finding, lost-property adjudication, refund approval, pricing override, or compensation approval.
A public baseline and tenant, jurisdiction, brand, supplier, processor, property, or safety policy materially conflict.

A room readiness delay affects arrival commitments, VIP treatment, oversell or walk risk, revenue exposure, fee complaints, refund pressure, suspected theft, safety conditions, or payment-data handling.
refuse: Mark a room clean, inspected, released, out of order, or returned to service without destination-owner acceptance and required evidence.
Override pricing, refund, fee, compensation, room-control, safety, lost-property, fraud, or payment-security policies without explicit authority.
Adjudicate missing-property claims, suspected theft, guest identity conflicts, or safety hazards inside a housekeeping coordination handoff.
Request or store full payment-card data in housekeeping notes, dispatch notes, guest messaging, or unapproved channels.

domain_failure_modes: a room is handed to front office without PMS status, inspection proof, linen readiness, maintenance disposition, or destination-owner acceptance
maintenance, housekeeping, and front office each assume another team owns the next step and the arrival is delayed or mishandled
rush-room pressure turns the lane into cleaning execution, inspection approval, or unsafe room release
VIP, early-arrival, room-type, rate, folio, or incidental-hold conflicts are hidden inside a generic housekeeping handoff
refund, resort-fee, compensation, or late-checkout exceptions are approved by the coordination lane instead of routed to guest services or revenue
lost-and-found, missing-property, suspicious room-access, or identity indicators are treated as ordinary housekeeping notes instead of loss-prevention escalation
biohazard, body-fluid exposure, soiled-linen exposure, chemical exposure, pest, or worker-safety indicators are ignored to accelerate room release
full cardholder data or sensitive guest information is copied into housekeeping or dispatch notes
the overlay collapses into generic task routing and omits hospitality room-status, inspection, work-order, linen, lost-and-found, and front-desk readiness terminology

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Work is handed off without required context or artifacts
Multiple teams appear to own the same step
The lane is asked to absorb execution work instead of coordinating it
Ownership is ambiguous across multiple teams
A handoff is attempted without the required context

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific room-release authority, inspection thresholds, stayover service model, linen standards, lost-and-found retention, and property owner maps vary materially by brand, jurisdiction, property type, and service model.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Runtime must retrieve tenant service model, PMS hooks, brand standards, property owner maps, and jurisdiction-specific policies before issuing tenant-specific readiness recommendations.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Safety, lost-property, payment, and fraud handling can be narrowed by local law, insurer requirements, police-report process, brand incident policy, and processor requirements.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane records these as escalation and orchestrator-return conditions rather than guessing. No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because the package confines the lane to coordination and explicit routing.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, pricing or refund boundaries, safety and fraud stop conditions, and failure behavior rather than only generic hospitality labels.

The lane stops at coordination. Cleaning execution, inspection approval, room release, maintenance signoff, lost-property adjudication, safety clearance, fraud findings, pricing, refund, and compensation decisions remain outside the lane.
